message:
fix(abi): robust stdio buffering with partial write loop and real errno extraction

Improve stdio stream handling for correctness under real-world conditions:

- flush_stream(): Add partial write loop to handle short writes from
  the kernel (previously assumed full write on each syscall)
- fopen()/freopen(): Extract actual errno from syscall result instead
  of hardcoding ENOENT, ensuring callers see the real failure reason
  (EACCES, ENOMEM, etc.)
- putchar(): Refactor to delegate to fputc(STDOUT_SENTINEL) for
  buffering coherence with the rest of the stdio layer
- puts(): Use buffered fputs() + fputc() instead of direct syscalls
  to maintain consistency with fprintf/printf buffering model

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
